title: Specific Ricoh MFP and Printer Products - Cross-site scripting vulnerability(CVE-2022-37406) via Web Image monitor
explanation:
Ricoh has identified a vulnerability involving Cross-site scripting (CVE-2022-37406) via Web Image monitor.

 

List 1 below shows the affected products and services. Ricoh offers countermeasures detailed in the hyperlinked pages in the list.

 


This vulnerability could allow an attacker to execute arbitrary scripts on the Web browser of a user logged on with administrative privileges.
Please refer to the following URL for the details of the vulnerability.
https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2022-37406

 

Note:
We have identified an issue with the Ricoh Firmware Update Tool where firmware updates may fail when attempting to update multiple devices of the same model simultaneously.
This issue has been resolved in the latest version of the tool.
We kindly request that you ensure you are using the most recent version of the Ricoh Firmware Update Tool.
In the table below, devices that are likely to encounter the issue are marked with an asterisk.
contact: Please contact your local Ricoh representative or dealer if you have any queries.
authenticityGuarantee: Please note that any copy or paraphrase of the text of this document that differs in content from the distribution URL link, or omits the URL, is an uncontrolled copy and may lack important information or contain factual errors.
